headline: PayPal's Checkout Recovery: Can Branded TPV Reaccelerate?
summary: PYPL's branded checkout TPV grows 2% in Q1 2026, indicating stabilization as investors watch whether the recovery can build from here.

headline: PayPal Ad Executive Explains How Shoppable Ads Are Disrupting Retail
summary: PayPal Ads SVP & General Manager Mark Grether discusses the launch of the power of transaction-based shoppable ad units. Grether explains how consumer shopping habits are shifting away from traditional search engines toward artificial-intelligence models, and why trust remains a critical factor for older demographics.

headline: Buy, Hold, or Sell: PayPal Shed 46% as Wall Street Deserts Fintech. Is PYPL an Absolute Buy at $42?
summary: PayPal (NASDAQ:PYPL) at $42.51 looks compelling for research. The stock has lost 37.46% over the past year while the business kept generating cash, retiring shares, and growing payment volume, and that gap between price action and fundamentals is the trade. headline: This Week In Digital Payment - Enhancing Nordic Bank Security Through CGI Entersekt Partnership
summary: CGI and Entersekt have partnered to enhance 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication services for issuing banks in the Nordics, aiming to strengthen fraud prevention while ensuring seamless customer experiences. This collaboration enables CGI in Norway to offer Entersekt’s cloud-based 3DS Access Control Server, implementing advanced authentication technologies such as biometrics and adaptive risk-based decisioning. Notably, Norion Bank in Sweden is the first to implement this solution as part of its...

headline: PayPal Ads Delivers Bottom-Line Growth Advertisers Can Measure
summary: Marketers have spent years optimizing for attention, but impressions aren't what CFOs look for when they're deciding where to cut or double down. Brands like Adorama, Best Buy, and Etsy are turning to PayPal Ads because it's built on what people actually purchase, giving marketers a more direct path to measurable business outcomes.

headline: Google, Meta Unite With Crypto Analytics Firms At Prince William's Forum To Fight Illegal Wildlife Trade: Report
summary: Major technology and cryptocurrency companies pledged to combat the illegal wildlife trade during London Climate Action Week, according to a report released on Monday, June 21.
